title: Server provisioning — Node.js, nginx, PM2
description: Set up the EC2 instance to run any Node.js app. Install Node.js 20 LTS, bun, and git. Configure nginx as a reverse proxy (port 80 → 3000). Install PM2 and configure it as a systemd service for auto-restart on reboot. This is a reusable skill — same setup works for any Node.js deployment.

plan.text: SSH into instance, run `sudo dnf update -y` to update all system packages.
Install Node.js 20 LTS via NodeSource repo, install bun via official installer, install git.
Install nginx, create reverse proxy config at /etc/nginx/conf.d/unpack.conf (proxy_pass to localhost:3000, WebSocket/SSE headers), enable and start nginx.
acceptance_criteria: nginx is running as systemd service
SSE-specific headers set (proxy_buffering off, X-Accel-Buffering no)

plan.text: Install PM2 globally via npm, create ecosystem.config.cjs in the project root, run `pm2 startup` to configure systemd auto-start on reboot.
acceptance_criteria: ecosystem.config.cjs defines the app with NODE_ENV=production
Node.js 20 LTS, bun, and git installed on EC2
nginx running as reverse proxy (port 80 → 3000) with SSE support
PM2 installed and configured for auto-restart on reboot

context.notes: Shaky skill: Deployment — commands explained. Amazon Linux 2023 uses dnf. nginx config must disable proxy buffering for SSE streaming to work (proxy_buffering off). PM2 ecosystem file must use .cjs extension (CommonJS) since the project is ESM.
